'''Lyco Pibex''' were diminutive, passive creatures found throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. They were able to run at rapid paces, and were kept as pets by [[Spacer/Legends|spacers]]. At some point, two Lyco Pibex formed a colony in [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley]] [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]] on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], and came into conflict with the native [[Jawa/Legends|Jawa]] tribes there.

==Biology and appearance==
Lyco Pibex were small, [[Sentience/Legends|non-sentient]] creatures, approximately a [[Meter/Legends|meter]] in height. Their dual [[Limb/Legends|legs]] allowed them to run at a considerable pace, and they also possessed two arms with which they could manipulate and grasp objects. The skin of the Lyco Pibex was red in hue, and for nutrition, their diet included metal alloys.<ref name="daa" />

==Behavior==
Lyco Pibex were passive by nature, but also considered to be strange. For communication, they relied on a series of squeaks and hisses.<ref name="daa" />

==History==
Lyco Pibex were found throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. They were kept as domesticated pets by interstellar [[Spacer/Legends|spacers]]. At some stage, two Lyco Pibex brought to [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley]] on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] escaped, and were able to form a colony. Their tendency to consume the metal alloys that the local [[Jawa/Legends|Jawa]] scavengers often hoped to salvage brought them into direct conflict with the natives. As the decades wore on, the Jawas grew increasingly irritated with the Mos Eisley Lyco Pibex colony, and would even resort to firing their [[Ion blaster/Legends|ion blasters]] at the creatures to scare them away.<ref name="daa" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Lyco Pibex were created by [[Brendon Fraim]] through the "[[Design An Alien]]" competition held by the [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine|''Star Wars Galaxy'']] magazine. The competition was initiated in [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine 3|''Star Wars Galaxy Magazine'' 3]] ([[1995]]), where prospective contestants were asked to design aliens based on the universe of the [[Original trilogy|original]] ''[[Star Wars]]'' trilogy. Fraim, who was 22 years of age at the time, had his entry published in the "16 years old and up" division of the competition, which was displayed in [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine 7|''Star Wars Galaxy Magazine'' 7]] ([[1996]]). The entry for the Lyco Pibex came in second place in the division, after the [[Ubese Thorn-Back War Dragon]]. Continuity editor [[Leland Chee]] later confirmed that the published "Design an Alien" is [[canon]].<ref name="tt">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=690#14724917|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Leland Y Chee]]|date=2006-05-03|time=5:45 PM|quote=Star Wars Galaxy Magazine #7 published the winners of the "Design an Alien" contest, where fans were encouraged to submit artwork and backstories of new SW aliens. Are these aliens canon? [...] Yes, though any text discrepencies with other sources would likely to superceded by the other source.}}</ref> The Lyco Pibex have not been referenced in any later ''Star Wars'' works.
